title: SDK-Backed HTTP API Thin Wrapper Implementation Tasks
principles: Keep HTTP as transport: routes, status annotations, OpenAPI and envelopes only.
Use names that describe calculator API transport, calculator-run execution and report-only convenience.
Keep reusable calculator execution in the SDK and calculator service boundaries.
Reuse canonical CalculatorRun* schemas and CalculatorServiceError from @whattax/calculators.
Document current and target call graphs when runtime boundaries move.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit after each coherent package-facing slice passes mandatory verification and parent review.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: SDK import-boundary check proving no @whattax/http-api dependency
HTTP handler audit proving calculate delegates full-run execution to the SDK
Naming audit proving final docs/call graphs advertise CalculatorApi* and calculateRunRequest/calculateReportRequest names
evidenceRequired: Current and final call graph summary
Public naming migration summary and any transitional aliases
Changeset path and release-train impact, or explicit no-changeset rationale
Type-test evidence for SDK descriptor safety
Runtime parity evidence for SDK, calculator service and HTTP API

tasks.title: Rename API and SDK public symbols for clear call graphs
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

Before editing:

- Read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in `docs/architecture/*`.
- Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.
- Review and preserve the spec's call-graph diagrams; update them if implementation discovers a different final call graph.

Implementation rules:

- MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: `Data`, `Schema`, `Array`, `Chunk`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `Match`, `Context`, `Layer`, `Config`, `Service`, `Record`, `Result`, `Exit`, `Bun`, `Platform`, `Command` and `ManagedRuntime`.
- Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, `switch`, `Object.values`, `Object.entries`, ad hoc `_tag` objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
- Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
- Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.
- Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use `Effect.mapError`, `Effect.catchTag`, `Effect.catchAll` or `Effect.catchAllDefect`.
- Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
- Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with `bun run changeset`, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.

Verification and handoff:

- Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including `bun run verification` unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
- Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
- Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
- Report whether the final implementation still matches the spec's call graph.
- Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.

Task-specific scope: rename HTTP API public symbols from `PublicCalculationMetadataGroup`, `PublicCalculationMetadataHandlerLive`, `PublicErrorEnvelope` and `PublicErrorEnvelopeData` to `CalculatorApiGroup`, `CalculatorApiHandlerLive`, `CalculatorApiErrorEnvelope` and `CalculatorApiErrorEnvelopeData`. Rename SDK Effect report-only helpers/types from `calculateRequest`, `calculate`, `createEffectClient`, `SdkCalculationPayload` and `SdkCalculationServiceRequest` to `calculateReportRequest`, `calculateReport`, `createClient`, `SdkCalculatorRunPayload` and `SdkCalculatorRunServiceRequest`. Keep transitional aliases only if required for staged migration, mark them deprecated, and do not advertise them in docs/call graphs.
mandatoryVerification: rg audit proving preferred source/docs use CalculatorApi* and calculateReport* names
completionCriteria: HTTP calculator API call graphs use `CalculatorApiGroup` and `CalculatorApiHandlerLive` names.
HTTP error envelope names describe calculator API transport, not generic public exposure.
SDK Effect report-only helper names clearly say report-only.
Any old names retained as aliases are deprecated and not documented as preferred names.
Parent agent verified the Changeset and final naming call graph.

tasks.title: Add SDK Effect full-run helper
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

Before editing:

- Read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in `docs/architecture/*`.
- Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.
- Review and preserve the spec's call-graph diagrams; update them if implementation discovers a different final call graph.

Implementation rules:

- MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: `Data`, `Schema`, `Array`, `Chunk`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `Match`, `Context`, `Layer`, `Config`, `Service`, `Record`, `Result`, `Exit`, `Bun`, `Platform`, `Command` and `ManagedRuntime`.
- Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, `switch`, `Object.values`, `Object.entries`, ad hoc `_tag` objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
- Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
- Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.
- Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use `Effect.mapError`, `Effect.catchTag`, `Effect.catchAll` or `Effect.catchAllDefect`.
- Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
- Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with `bun run changeset`, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.

Verification and handoff:

- Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including `bun run verification` unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
- Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
- Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
- Report whether the final implementation still matches the spec's call graph.
- Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.

Task-specific scope: add an `@whattax/sdk/effect` full-run helper, `calculateRunRequest`, that calls `PublicCalculatorService.calculate`, decodes the descriptor output report and returns the canonical calculator response shape with report typing narrowed to the descriptor output. Make `calculateReportRequest` and `calculateReport` delegate through it. Do not edit HTTP handler behavior in this task.
mandatoryVerification: rg audit proving report-only SDK helpers delegate through the full-run helper
completionCriteria: SDK Effect facade exposes `calculateRunRequest` with descriptor-narrowed report typing.
No SDK dependency on @whattax/http-api is introduced.
No duplicated calculator-run runtime schema or mirrored DTO is introduced.
SDK runtime and type tests cover the full-run helper.
Parent agent verified the Changeset and call graph.

tasks.title: Make HTTP calculate delegate to SDK full-run helper
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

Before editing:

- Read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in `docs/architecture/*`.
- Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.
- Review and preserve the spec's call-graph diagrams; update them if implementation discovers a different final call graph.

Implementation rules:

- MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: `Data`, `Schema`, `Array`, `Chunk`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `Match`, `Context`, `Layer`, `Config`, `Service`, `Record`, `Result`, `Exit`, `Bun`, `Platform`, `Command` and `ManagedRuntime`.
- Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, `switch`, `Object.values`, `Object.entries`, ad hoc `_tag` objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
- Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
- Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.

- Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
- Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with `bun run changeset`, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.

- Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including `bun run verification` unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
- Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.

Task-specific scope: update the HTTP calculate handler so it calls `calculateRunRequest` once and maps only transport envelopes. The calculate handler must not call `getCalculator`, `getCalculatorGraph` or construct `CalculatorRunResponseData`. Preserve metadata/list/schema/graph route behavior.
mandatoryVerification: rg audit proving HTTP calculate handler does not call getCalculator/getCalculatorGraph or construct CalculatorRunResponseData
completionCriteria: HTTP calculate delegates full-run execution to the SDK.
HTTP still owns route schemas, status annotations, OpenAPI metadata and CalculatorApiErrorEnvelope.
HTTP success and expected error behavior remain compatible.
Parent agent verified no SDK dependency on @whattax/http-api.
Parent agent verified the Changeset and call graph.

Task-specific scope: update architecture docs, package READMEs and downstream validation evidence for the final thin HTTP-over-SDK call graph and final public names. Keep public WhatTax docs neutral and do not name private downstream product details.
outputs: Downstream validation evidence recorded in the handoff or active exec plan
mandatoryVerification: downstream workspace typecheck command if vendored SDK/API consumption changes
downstream browser/build check if SDK/API consumption changes in browser code
rg audit proving docs include final call graph and final public names
bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale
completionCriteria: Architecture docs include the final production and test call graphs.

Public docs remain neutral and do not name private downstream product details.
Parent agent accepted the Changeset or no-changeset rationale.
